license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iv9ce96
title: Guava Flow Cytometer - Troubleshooting "Tray Hold Off State"
author: Steven W Wilhelm

steps_raw.instruction: Open Millipore software to "home" (not in cleaning, InCyte, etc.)
Remove flow cell The flow cell will break if it is not removed
Power off the flow cytometer (FCM) using the toggle switch on the back of the machine
Unscrew two torx screws in the tray bay at the top left and right, under the door to remove the top panel of the machine. Torx size T - 10
Unplug the power supply and USB connection on the back of the FCM
Lift the front of the hood to >45º, and slide the hood back away from the machine to free the back of the hood.
Pull the sampling tray forward to the "eject" position This step may require some force

Reset the "motor/sensor" by carefully pushing the sensor to the bottom There is an extra 'motor' in the drawer under the FCM in bubble wrap. Pull this out to practice moving the assembly prior to manipulating the machine. The sensor is marked as A on the extra motor.

Push the sensor part all of the way up Take care not the bend/break the thin metal piece on the bottom of the sensor. You shoild manipulate the motor by pushing or pulling the black "L" shaped portion or the screw. Do not push/pull the metal strip to move the sensor.
Move the sampling tray as far back as it will go
Replace the hood in the opposite order stated in steps 4-7 You may wish to replace the screws after the machine initiates
If FCM initiates (i.e., gears are moving etc.) continue; if not, check to make sure that the sample tray and sensor are in the correct positions (i.e., all the way to the back and top, respectively).
Place the system in capillary shutdown mode
